title: info: add rootless field
body:
Add a rootless field to the info data (e.g., `podman info`) to indicate
if the executing user is root or not.  In most cases, this can be
guessed but now it is clear and may aid in debugging, reporting and
understanding certain issues.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

comments.body:
@giuseppe PTAL

The intention behind is to facilitate reading bug reports. Sometimes, it's unclear if the user was running as root or not. Now, we can see it instantly.

It's funny I was considering doing this yesterday, for this reason, since we often miss the fact that podman is running rootless.  I thought it was easy enough to figure it out, so I decided against it, but since a second person thought of doing this, I think it is worth it.

/lgtm

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> It's funny I was considering doing this yesterday, for this reason, since we often miss the fact that podman is running rootless. I thought it was easy enough to figure it out, so I decided against it, but since a second person thought of doing this, I think it is worth it.

Maybe we were triggered by the same issue on Github :) I was wondering if buildah-info would make sense as well? Given with RHEL 7.6 it started sailing on enterprise waters, this can come in handy for bug reports, log aggregation, etc. (Cc @TomSweeneyRedHat)
Late to the LGTM party, but I'll throw that down and a nice idea.  I'm not sure how easily we can do this in `buildah info`, but will take a look.
